faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Belleville home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool garments, carpets, or upholstery; silken tubes or cases on fabrics; adult moths flying around closets or storage areas; and webbing on fabric surfaces. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on stored clothing or in dark corners of closets. Our Belleville technicians can conduct a thorough inspection to confirm the presence and extent of any infestation.

faqs.question: What determines the cost of clothes moth control in Belleville?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550 and $5,000 depends on several factors including the size of your property, the severity of the infestation, the number of treatment areas needed, and whether specialized fumigation or heat treatments are required. A single-room treatment in a small apartment will cost significantly less than whole-house treatment in a larger Belleville home with multiple closets and storage areas. We provide detailed estimates after our initial inspection.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and belongings in Belleville?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe for your family, pets, and fabrics when applied correctly. Our treatments target clothes moths specifically while protecting your valuable garments and textiles. We provide clear pre-treatment and post-treatment instructions to ensure maximum effectiveness and safety. Many of our solutions include non-chemical options like heat treatment, freezing, and pheromone traps.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Belleville home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in sealed containers, dry-cleaning garments before long-term storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and ensuring proper ventilation in closets. We provide customized prevention plans for Belleville residents and offer follow-up inspections to ensure your home remains moth-free. Regular monitoring and maintenance are key to long-term protection.
